conversation_context.user_goal: Implement the next 02-read table slice by running the pending table-cell-semantics probes, tightening the production blocker, and only enabling table read if exact ordering closes.
user_constraints: Stay inside Hancom Docs Automation SDK scope.
Use raw CDP and separate worker Chrome profiles for live validation.
Do not promote fallback or lossy read behavior as final capability.
Do not turn current caret formatting into a new public capability.
decisions: Paragraph style belongs in existing SDK paragraph outputs (`ParagraphBlock.paragraphStyle`, `getParagraphFormatting().paragraphStyle`), not a selection API.
Mixed paragraphs keep `paragraphStyleConsistent=false`, but block-level `paragraphStyle` may still expose field-level consensus for shared `alignment` and/or `lineSpacing`.
Current-caret `HwpApp.hwpCaret.CSr.M3e.nge` evidence is retained only to reject stale document paths, not as the SDK source of truth.
Current runtime re-check does not support a second paragraph-text branch for this document: `Zvr.$bi.G0i` mirrors body and `Svr._Vi.G0i` is break-node-only.
Baseline doc `2025년+사회조사+결과+보도자료+2.hwp` now strips residual separator word `31` in production read paths, including the `space + 31 + space -> single space` case.
Table cell semantics should be narrowed with exact runtime joins and structural signatures before any row/col/cell labels are promoted.
Synthetic production `TableBlock` emission stays disabled until stable row/cell ordering is proven from live runtime evidence.
Production warnings should distinguish `o6n/selection bridge unavailable` from `stable row/cell ordering unresolved`.
Table step artifacts should carry a compact fingerprint so current carrier rotation can be compared without reparsing the full raw carrier array.
Saved table walk artifacts should be normalized into contiguous phase segments before inferring any matrix ordering semantics.
Tab-order labeling probes may be used for discovery, but they only prove alignment to editor traversal order, not absolute row-major semantics.
Arrow-key probes can produce shape-dependent divergence where semantics carriers move but current cell text stays stale; treat them as correlation evidence, not source of truth.
conversation_context.latest_user_request: Continue the 02-read track and narrow table ordering evidence with additional live probes.
open_questions: Which field or field combination distinguishes current row/col/cell neighborhood from broader traversal phase without relying on `_ie.length`, and can `carrierLineage` be aligned to a stable cell matrix?
Do rectangular tables (`2x3` vs `3x2`) imply that `selectionCarrier` registry-family shifts, rather than `z5n`, carry the first stable row/col signal?
Can the normalized phase segments (`baseline`, `overflow`, `reverse-mid`) be mapped to row-major or column-major movement without assuming `_ie` cardinality semantics?
Can the new Tab-order-labeled carrier families be reproduced under non-Tab movement (arrow keys or click seeds), so they can be promoted beyond traversal-order evidence?
Why does `2x3` Arrow movement let carrier families change while `pageReadCurrentTableCellState()` text stays on `T01`, while `3x2` Arrow movement tracks labels more closely?

subtasks.notes: Paragraph-level snapshot now exists via `pageProbeParagraphStyles()`: `paraStyleRefs[]`, `paragraphStyleConsistent`, resolved `paragraphStyle`, `paraStyleVariants[]`, `rawNodeIds[]`, and inline `runs[]`. `pageProbeAoiPlaceholderSpans()` reconstructs the strongest placeholder family as exact `Aoi` offset ranges and removes embedded object payload from mixed nodes. `pageProbeAoiControlPrefixes()` and `pageProbeAoiBucketSemantics()` isolate the remaining non-placeholder buckets: doc2 leading `31` family clusters around section-heading context, while doc1/doc2 leading `11` family clusters around tail-side layout/control context. `pageProbeAoiSectionMarkerRewrite()` now shows sampled `31` family nodes remain meaningful after removing word `31`. Remaining blockers are a canonical span metadata path and a canonical metadata path for promoting that strip rule.

subtasks.notes: Recovered exact registry/control joins plus forward/reverse traversal evidence. `z0i -> o6n.zli` target sets now diff per step, table-cell-semantics artifacts expose a compact `fingerprint` (`currentCarrier`, `carrierLineage`, `ieHeadSignature`, `z5nSignature`), and the regenerated runs show `ieHeadSignature`/`z5nSignature` staying mostly fixed while current carrier lineage rotates. Rectangular housing reruns (`2x3`, `3x2`) strengthened this split: `z5nStructures` stayed identical across both dimensions, while the `selectionCarriers -> registryMatches` family changed dimension-sensitively after overflow/reverse. New pure analysis helper/artifact now normalize those saved walks into contiguous phase segments and show the rectangular runs sharing only the baseline family while diverging in overflow/reverse families. New tab-order labeling probe writes `T00..` into fresh cells and ties those same families directly to editor traversal order; it shows reverse walk reading `T05 -> T00` consistently while `o6n[19]`, `o6n[56]`, `o6n[58]` attach to different tab-order slots across `2x3` and `3x2`. New Arrow probe adds non-Tab evidence: `3x2` Arrow movement changes both labels and carrier families, while `2x3` can change carrier families with stale cell text, so Arrow artifacts are useful correlation evidence but not yet canonical. `_ie` cardinality remains history-sensitive (`5 -> 48`, all-`48`, or `5 -> 12` depending on document/run history). Placeholder-span and bucket-semantic probes separate the doc2 section-marker `31` family from the doc1/doc2 tail-side `11` family, and section-marker rewrite probe shows sampled `31` nodes remain meaningful after removing control word `31`. Remaining blockers are canonical metadata for promoting that rewrite, canonical span metadata path, and exact row/col/cell labeling for the table registry fields.

needs_from: reconcile static Ivr.$5n paragraph snapshots with live HwpApp.hwpCaret.CSr.M3e.nge paragraph cache on mixed paragraphs
subtasks.notes: Core formatting semantics are now mutation-backed: visible font color is `runCharStyle.$Xt` (`#000000 -> 0`, `#FF0000 -> 255`, `#0000FF -> 16711680` in same-node replay), bold is `runCharStyle.cUt 5110 <-> 5108`, italic is `runCharStyle.cUt 5104 <-> 5105`, and same-paragraph anchored replay now confirms `HwpApp.hwpCaret.CSr.M3e.nge.cUt 260/268/264/384 -> left/center/right/both`. The same body-paragraph replay also showed node `styleRef -> Ivr.$5n` staying stale (`cUt=384`, `FNi=40`) while `CSr.M3e.nge` matched the toolbar (`cUt`, `FNi`). Current runtime re-check does not support a second paragraph-text branch for this document: `Zvr.$bi.G0i` mirrors `Svr.G0i` and `Svr._Vi.G0i` is break-node-only. `Xli` stays raw-only. The major paragraph-branch uncertainty is now resolved; the remaining risk is body-paragraph sample coverage for center/right plus the older clean-text/table blockers.
